ops.title: C1/H6 chosen architecture (codex-vetted): a custom default-deny Go socket proxy (pkgs/hermes-docker-proxy), NOT tecnativa (verb-only, can't screen create bodies) and NOT opa-docker-authz (keeps the raw socket reachable -> containerd/Content-Length/CVE bypass class). Key reasoning: gVisor does NOT stop bind-mount exfiltration (the gofer honours -v /:/host even under runsc), so a real fix must screen HostConfig at the API layer AND remove the raw socket from the agent. Proxy delegates transport to net/http/httputil (battle-tested incl. exec hijack); only the route allowlist + create-body screen are bespoke + unit-tested. Wiring: hermes dropped from docker group; dedicated hermes-docker-proxy user owns the real socket, primary group hermes so the 0660 filtered socket is agent-connectable; DOCKER_HOST points the agent at it. LIVE-TEST GATE: docker run -v /:/host from a real code-exec session must be refused while normal code-exec works; widen HERMES_DOCKER_PROXY_BIND_ROOTS if a legit mount source is outside stateDir. Residual TOCTOU on symlink bind sources (full close needs root-owned bind-root parents, which conflicts with the agent creating task dirs). Rootless docker (pin the hermes uid) is the construction-level follow-up.
